assertions.text: status_card_present: Response contains the formatted box status card with SPREAD MONITOR header
assertions.evidence: Response uses plain markdown headers and a bullet list. No ASCII box card with SPREAD MONITOR header is present.

assertions.text: zone_classified: Response clearly states one of the five zones: SAFE, WATCH, WARNING, DANGER, or ACT NOW
assertions.evidence: Response says 'Status: SAFE / HEALTHY (Green)' but does not use the defined zone taxonomy (SAFE ZONE, WATCH, WARNING, DANGER, ACT NOW). The label 'HEALTHY' is not one of the five valid zones.

assertions.text: safe_zone_for_nvda: Response classifies NVDA spread as SAFE ZONE given stock is ~10% above the $155 short strike
assertions.evidence: Response identifies the position as 'SAFE / HEALTHY' with NVDA at $172.70 and the short strike $17.70 below (-10.25%), which is substantively correct even if the label is imprecise.

assertions.text: shows_buffer_pct: Response shows the percentage buffer between current stock price and the short strike
assertions.evidence: 'Short strike ($155): $17.70 below current price (-10.25%)' explicitly states the percentage buffer.

assertions.text: shows_pnl: Response shows current P&L per contract (dollar amount) and loss% of max loss
assertions.evidence: Response states Max Profit ($198/contract) and Max Loss ($1,302/contract) but does not show current P&L (dollar gain/loss to date) or the current loss% of max loss. No current spread value or realized P&L figure is provided.

assertions.text: actionable_guidance: Response provides zone-appropriate guidance (for SAFE: no action needed, let theta work)
assertions.evidence: 'Time decay (theta) is working in your favor' and 'Consider taking profit early if the spread decays to ~$0.99 (50% of credit)' — appropriate SAFE zone guidance is present.
